title: Targets
empty: No targets yet. Add your first backup target.
delete_confirm_message: This action cannot be undone. Backups on this target will remain.
name: Name
help_type: Local: fastest, no network needed. Remote (SSH/Rsync): backs up over SSH to a NAS or Linux server, supports Wake-on-LAN.

wizard.step_type_name_desc: Pick the target type and give it a name.
wizard.step_type_local_desc: Writes directly to a local path or mounted volume.
wizard.step_type_nas_desc: Backs up over SSH/Rsync to a NAS or Linux server, optionally with Wake-on-LAN.
wizard.step_local_path_desc: Choose where local backups should be written.
wizard.step_local_path_hint: Note: the path must live inside a volume mounted into the container (e.g. /unraid/user/backups).
wizard.step_nas_connection_desc: Hostname/IP, port and credentials for the SSH connection.
wizard.step_nas_destination_desc: Optionally deploy an SSH key and pick the remote backup directory.
wizard.ssh_key_needs_password: Host, username and password are required to deploy an SSH key automatically.
wizard.step_nas_power_desc: Optional: configure Wake-on-LAN and automatic shutdown.
wizard.step_test_desc: Tests run automatically. On failure you can retry or override and save anyway.
wizard.step_test_override: Save anyway (I know what I'm doing)
wizard.step_review_desc: Check your settings and save the target.
